title: hieracium nigrescens subsp. odontolipes
intro: Hieracium nigrescens subsp. odontolipes, also known as the Black Hawk's Claw, is a subspecies of the Hieracium nigrescens plant, which belongs to the dandelion family and is characterized by its dark stems and toothed leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Hieracium nigrescens subsp. odontolipes include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used externally in the form of creams and ointments for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
